error.signature: AI tells someone in Indonesia to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Indonesia does not use 911. The unified emergency number is 112 (free from mobiles in most regions). You can also dial 110 for police, 113 for fire, and 118 or 119 for ambulance.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Indonesia expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Indonesia. The unified number is 112; police is 110, fire is 113, and ambulance is 118/119. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume 112 works identically everywhere in Indonesia
dead_ends.why_fails: 112 coverage is strong in major cities but can be inconsistent in remote areas. Where 112 does not connect, fall back to 110/113/118.

workarounds.action: If 112 does not connect, use the service-specific numbers: 110 police, 113 fire, 118/119 ambulance.
workarounds.how: Try 112 first; if it fails, dial the specific service number for your area.
workarounds.tradeoff: Service-specific numbers require you to pick the right one.
